## Summary

I extracted **7 program graphs** from the Osaka `blocks.py` specification at `https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/forks/amsterdam/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/blocks.py`.

### Functional Units Identified

| ID | Name | Type | Description |
|:---|:-----|:-----|:------------|
| SG-001 | `encode_receipt` | Function | Encodes a receipt with a type-prefix byte based on transaction type (5-way branch: AccessList/FeeMarket/Blob/SetCode/Legacy) |
| SG-002 | `decode_receipt` | Function | Decodes a receipt from serialized bytes, stripping the type prefix and RLP-decoding, or returning non-Bytes receipts as-is |
| SG-003 | `withdrawal_construction` | Data Structure | Construction of an immutable `Withdrawal` dataclass (4 fields: index, validator_index, address, amount) |
| SG-004 | `header_construction` | Data Structure | Construction of an immutable `Header` dataclass (21 fields covering consensus, state, and EIP metadata) |
| SG-005 | `block_construction` | Data Structure | Construction of an immutable `Block` dataclass (header, transactions, ommers, withdrawals) |
| SG-006 | `log_construction` | Data Structure | Construction of an immutable `Log` dataclass (address, topics, data) |
| SG-007 | `receipt_construction` | Data Structure | Construction of an immutable `Receipt` dataclass (succeeded, cumulative_gas_used, bloom, logs) |

### Files Written

All `.mmd` files are in `/home/gohan/ga-runners/actions-runner-security-agent-2/_work/security-agent/security-agent/outputs/graphs/W3B4_1770278556/osaka-blocks/`:

- `SG-001_encode_receipt.mmd` -- 5-way type dispatch with RLP encoding
- `SG-002_decode_receipt.mmd` -- Bytes vs Receipt type check with assertion guard
- `SG-003_withdrawal_construction.mmd` -- Linear 4-field construction
- `SG-004_header_construction.mmd` -- Linear 21-field construction
- `SG-005_block_construction.mmd` -- Linear 4-field construction
- `SG-006_log_construction.mmd` -- Linear 3-field construction
- `SG-007_receipt_construction.mmd` -- Linear 4-field construction

### Key Observations

- **`encode_receipt`** is the most complex graph with a 5-way branch based on transaction type, each branch producing a different type-prefix byte (0x01-0x04) or returning the raw receipt for legacy transactions.
- **`decode_receipt`** contains an assertion guard (`assert receipt[0] in (1, 2, 3, 4)`) that acts as a runtime invariant check -- if the first byte is not a valid type prefix, the function will fail.
- All data structures use `@slotted_freezable @dataclass`, meaning they are immutable after construction. This is captured in the program graphs as a terminal `freeze()` action.

## Summary

I extracted **7 program graphs** from the Osaka `blocks.py` specification at `https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/forks/amsterdam/src/ethereum/forks/osaka/blocks.py`.

### Functional Units Identified

| ID | Name | Type | Description |
|:---|:-----|:-----|:------------|
| SG-001 | `encode_receipt` | Function | Encodes a receipt with a type-prefix byte based on transaction type (5-way branch: AccessList/FeeMarket/Blob/SetCode/Legacy) |
| SG-002 | `decode_receipt` | Function | Decodes a receipt from serialized bytes, stripping the type prefix and RLP-decoding, or returning non-Bytes receipts as-is |
| SG-003 | `withdrawal_construction` | Data Structure | Construction of an immutable `Withdrawal` dataclass (4 fields: index, validator_index, address, amount) |
| SG-004 | `header_construction` | Data Structure | Construction of an immutable `Header` dataclass (21 fields covering consensus, state, and EIP metadata) |
| SG-005 | `block_construction` | Data Structure | Construction of an immutable `Block` dataclass (header, transactions, ommers, withdrawals) |
| SG-006 | `log_construction` | Data Structure | Construction of an immutable `Log` dataclass (address, topics, data) |
| SG-007 | `receipt_construction` | Data Structure | Construction of an immutable `Receipt` dataclass (succeeded, cumulative_gas_used, bloom, logs) |

### Files Written

All `.mmd` files are in `/home/gohan/ga-runners/actions-runner-security-agent-2/_work/security-agent/security-agent/outputs/graphs/W3B4_1770278556/osaka-blocks/`:

- `SG-001_encode_receipt.mmd` -- 5-way type dispatch with RLP encoding
- `SG-002_decode_receipt.mmd` -- Bytes vs Receipt type check with assertion guard
- `SG-003_withdrawal_construction.mmd` -- Linear 4-field construction
- `SG-004_header_construction.mmd` -- Linear 21-field construction
- `SG-005_block_construction.mmd` -- Linear 4-field construction
- `SG-006_log_construction.mmd` -- Linear 3-field construction
- `SG-007_receipt_construction.mmd` -- Linear 4-field construction

### Key Observations

- **`encode_receipt`** is the most complex graph with a 5-way branch based on transaction type, each branch producing a different type-prefix byte (0x01-0x04) or returning the raw receipt for legacy transactions.
- **`decode_receipt`** contains an assertion guard (`assert receipt[0] in (1, 2, 3, 4)`) that acts as a runtime invariant check -- if the first byte is not a valid type prefix, the function will fail.
- All data structures use `@slotted_freezable @dataclass`, meaning they are immutable after construction. This is captured in the program graphs as a terminal `freeze()` action.
